label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So, the question is whether following these steps will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward. Then take 2 steps backward, 5 steps backward, 5 steps right, and 9 steps right. The options are yes or no. First, I need to visualize this. Let's assume I start at a point, let's call it (0,0) on a coordinate system. Facing forward means my initial direction is, say, along the positive y-axis. But when I take steps backward, that would be in the negative y-direction, right? Because if I'm facing forward (up the y-axis), stepping backward is down
